body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall experience in the Finance & Accounting program at the School of Business Administration during this academic year has been acceptable, with meaningful highs and some notable lows that I believe the institution has both the capacity and responsibility to address. The academic content, when delivered well, is genuinely rigorous and relevant, and I have formed valuable relationships with peers and some excellent faculty members. However, structural issues — inconsistent teaching quality, administrative inefficiencies, and gaps in student support — have at times undermined what could otherwise be an exceptional educational experience. With targeted improvements and a genuine commitment to acting on student feedback, this institution has the foundations to become truly outstanding.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Business Administration has been outstanding and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I value the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Finance & Accounting curriculum at the School of Business Administration appears to lag behind current industry standards in several significant areas that I believe require urgent review and revision. Many courses continue to teach outdated tools and methodologies that are rarely used in professional settings today, leaving students underprepared for actual workplace demands. The curriculum also lacks sufficient elective depth and specialization pathways, limiting students' ability to develop expertise in niche areas of growing importance. I recommend the Academic Affairs Office and school leadership to conduct a thorough curriculum audit in close consultation with industry professionals and alumni to ensure course content remains relevant, rigorous, and career-oriented.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: Extracurricular opportunities at the School of Business Administration, managed by the Student Affairs Office, are insufficient and poorly promoted, limiting student engagement with the broader campus community significantly. The range of active student clubs is narrow, with minimal support for students wishing to establish new interest groups or activities that reflect the diversity of the student population. Events are inconsistently organized and inadequately funded, resulting in low attendance and limited impact on the student community's sense of belonging and engagement. I strongly flag the Student Affairs Office to take a more proactive and well-resourced approach to extracurricular programming, recognizing its critical role in student retention and overall satisfaction.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Business Administration has been superb and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I appreciate the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services provided by the Finance Department at the School of Business Administration have been commendable this semester, and I genuinely applaud the clarity, transparency, and efficiency with which they are managed. Fee statements are issued promptly and accurately, with clear breakdowns of tuition, facility charges, and any applicable scholarship deductions that make it easy to verify and plan payments. Payment options are flexible and well-communicated, including installment plans that meaningfully reduce financial pressure on students and families. Scholarship disbursements are processed on time and confirmed through official notifications, removing uncertainty around critical financial support. The finance office staff are professional, patient, and genuinely helpful when navigating complex financial queries.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is remarkable and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Business Administration this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I acknowledge the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: Learning resources for my Finance & Accounting program at the School of Business Administration are moderate, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: The academic administrative processes at the School of Business Administration, overseen by the Academic Affairs Office, have been a source of considerable frustration for many students this semester. Course registration was plagued by system errors and conflicting timetables that took weeks to resolve, causing students to miss early enrollment advantages. Grade dispute processes are opaque and slow, with students waiting over a month without updates or acknowledgment of their submissions. Communication from administrative offices is reactive rather than proactive, and staff sometimes provide inconsistent information depending on who is contacted. I suggest a comprehensive review of administrative workflows, with investment in both system improvements and staff training as a matter of urgency.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My key recommendation for the School of Business Administration is to urgently address the problematic systemic issues that have affected Finance & Accounting students this semester, rather than treating them as isolated incidents. A structured, transparent student feedback mechanism — where concerns are acknowledged within a set timeframe and outcomes communicated back to students — would significantly rebuild trust that has been eroded by unaddressed issues. I also strongly point out leadership to conduct a comprehensive curriculum and service quality review in genuine consultation with current students, alumni, and industry partners. Without meaningful action on the concerns raised in this feedback, student satisfaction and the institution's competitive standing will continue to decline in the years ahead.
